issue.tagline: 追随建造者，而非追随影响者

lead.title: 模型吃掉 Harness：Google 把代理框架做成贯穿 50 款产品的中枢，Sakana 用「模型混部」撕开新架构
paras: 本期 Training Data 播客把话筒递给 Google AI Studio 负责人 Logan Kilpatrick，核心论点相当激进：「模型正在吃掉 Harness。」Logan 揭示了 Google IO 上力推的 Antigravity 远不只是 IDE——它是一个跨 Gemini API、Search、Gemini App、Cloud、AI Studio 的统一代理执行框架。Google 内部 50 多款产品正通过这一 harness「重新挂载」为代理原生产品，Gemini 模型从过去的「贯穿线」变成了 harness 内部的执行者。这意味着代理框架正在取代模型本身，成为产品层竞争的真正主战场。

与此同时，Aaron Levie 重点推荐了 Sakana 最新发布的 Fugu 模型——它不再是一个单体模型，而是一个能根据任务自动选择、分发、验证子模型的「模型路由器」：对外暴露单一 API，内部把工作分派给最擅长的小模型。Levie 评价这是「推动 AI 架构向前的新思路」。同一条线索上，Peter Yang 转发了 liu8in 的洞察：「智能体没有视觉智能，所以我们转向代码——HTML 是 LLM 的母语。」三种思路异曲同工：当单模型能力逼近上限，下一阶段的胜负手在编排层、路由层、表达层。

podcast.label: 本期播客
podcast.title: Logan Kilpatrick：模型正在吃掉 Harness
paras: Google AI Studio 与 Gemini API 负责人 Logan Kilpatrick 在最新一期 Training Data 中抛出令人警醒的判断：智能体时代的真正主战场不是模型，而是 harness（代理执行框架）。他分享了一个内部故事——在台上和 Gemini 模型负责人 Tulsi 同台时，台下观众用 Omni 实时把一张图里的狗编辑进画面，模型对「狗跳上膝、观众轻笑、嘉宾抚摸」这些微妙细节的处理让他震惊于「世界理解」的真实存在。

Logan 把 IO 上的 Antigravity 定义为一个完整生态：核心 IDE、Web 端代理优先体验、CLI、SDK，外加一个托管代理的 Gemini API。其杀手锏是同一套 harness 同时驱动 Search、Gemini App、Cloud、AI Studio——「全球只有两家公司需要在十亿用户产品里部署 Gemini，而我们有 13 个这样的产品」。他认为把 Gemini API 当成「贯穿线」的时代已经过去，现在是 harness 把所有产品粘合在一起。最后他给开发者的建议是：去理解 harness、贡献 harness，而不仅仅是调用 API。

items.name: Aaron Levie
items.text: Another new idea to push the state of AI architectures forward. Sakana released a model that effectively uses a mixture of models to get work done. You get a single API but then the work gets farmed out [to] the model that best performs the task. "Fugu manages model selection, delegation, verification, and training so that developers get a single API call that performs better than any single frontier model."

items.name: Aaron Levie
items.text: Agents will use software 100X more than people. When that happens, there's a huge need for guardrails on what the agents are doing so they don't leak data or change the wrong information, authoritative sources of truth for them to work with, logging and auditing of what they're doing...

items.name: Guillermo Rauch
items.text: Coding agents will squeeze every ounce of IKEA effect out of you, if you let them.

items.name: Thibault Sottiaux
items.text: What should we improve in the Codex app. What's not delightful?

items.name: Garry Tan
items.text: I think one underestimated thing when we look back on it was how useful it is to have your own personal brain and company brain in 2026 at the dawn of usable AGI. AGI gives you the intelligence. You still have to collect your personal context to get the real unlock.

items.name: Nan Yu
items.text: "Quality is irrational" is a great way to describe it. You have to have an irrational level of commitment to constantly choose quality and an irrational level of self-belief that controlling things top to bottom will yield better results than using common frameworks.

items.name: Ryo Lu
items.text: missing wooden shelves so i made Books in ryOS. started in Cursor mobile, then hand-tuned the animations and textures until things felt right.

items.name: Zara Zhang
items.text: A good rule of thumb for preventing AI slop (in writing, design, etc): Is your input (the context) longer than the output? I've found that for the AI to produce quality results, my input is often 3-5 times the length of the output.

colophon.label: 关于
colophon.text: AI Builders Digest · 每日精选 · 追随建造者而非追随影响者
